channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Cali Rep. Chu says 'wildfires have no political affiliations' after Trump floated conditions for federal aid
item.description: Rep. Judy Chu said she does not believe conditions should be placed on federal aid for wildfires sweeping through Southern California, after President Donald Trump suggested such.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's most vulnerable nominees RFK Jr, Tulsi Gabbard get back-to-back hearings
item.description: Two of Trump's most controversial picks for his administration are set to face Senate committees during the same week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: List: All of Trump’s executive orders on the economy
item.description: President Donald Trump started his second administration with a flurry of policy actions since returning to the White House on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Alaskans say Trump can change the name of Denali but can't make people call it Mount McKinley
item.description: President Donald Trump has changed the name of Alaska's Denali back to Mount McKinley. It's a nod to President William McKinley, who was from Ohio and who never set foot in Alaska. Many Alaskans say they'll never stop calling the mountain Denali.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: As Hegseth takes charge at the Pentagon, here's what changes could be in store
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth starts his first official day on Monday morning, he will face a daunting array of issues to tackle — from global conflicts and border security to administrative tasks.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s Palestinian refugee idea falls flat with Arab allies and confounds a Republican senator
item.description: President Donald Trump’s push to have Egypt and Jordan take in large numbers of Palestinian refugees from besieged Gaza has fallen flat with both countries' governments and perplexed a congressional ally.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump strikes back with retaliatory measures against Colombia for rejecting deportation flights
item.description: President Donald Trump has imposed strict consequences for Colombia after it refused to accept deportation flights from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump floats gutting the IRS, moving agents to the border armed with guns
item.description: President Donald Trump floated moving the thousands of IRS agents hired under the Biden administration to the southern border to help patrol the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ramaswamy's expected run for Ohio governor would test experienced Republicans and tradition
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Fresh off of his unexpected departure from President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, Vivek Ramaswamy has set his sights on becoming governor of his home state.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Secretary of State Rubio hails release of US prisoner in Belarus as controversy hangs over nation's election
item.description: President Donald Trump's new Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced that a U.S. citizen imprisoned in Belarus under Joe Biden has since been released.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's border emergency declaration comes amid relative calm after years of major turmoil
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Long stretches of silence on a Border Patrol scanner are punctuated with updates on tracking a single migrant for hours. The radio traffic sounds like a throwback to earlier times, before the United States became the largest destination for asylum-seekers in 2017.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, GREGORY BULL and EUGENE GARCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CIA releases new analysis on COVID origins favoring lab leak theory
item.description: The CIA has changed its assessment on the origins of the COVID-19 pandemic, now favoring the lab leak theory. But some worry this could be an indication of politicization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida Republican urges Trump to spare some migrants under deportation plan
item.description: Salazar, a daughter of Cuban exiles, represents Florida's 27th Congressional District which is home to a number of Venezuelans and Cubans.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Nikki Fried vows to rebuild Florida Democrats as re-elected chair
item.description: Nikki Fried was reelected Saturday as chair of the Florida Democratic Party, which is seeking to regain relevance in a state that has tilted decidedly toward the Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on Bessent nomination to lead Treasury
item.description: The Senate on Saturday voted to advance Scott Bessent’s nomination to become the next secretary of the Treasury, putting him in line to be confirmed on Monday. Senators voted 67-23 to tee up final passage on Bessent’s nomination. The final vote will take place Monday evening at 5:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: He's emboldened, he's organized and he's still Trump: Takeaways from the president's opening days
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s first week in office isn't over yet, but already it offers signals about how his next four years in the White House may unfold.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China tells Rubio to behave himself in veiled warning
item.description: The short phrase seemed aimed at Rubio's vocal criticism of China and its human rights record when he was a U.S. senator.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth confirmed as Trump's defense secretary in tie-breaking vote despite turmoil over his conduct
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed Pete Hegseth as the nation's defense secretary late Friday in a dramatic tie-breaking vote, swatting back questions about his qualifications to lead the Pentagon amid allegations of heavy drinking and aggressive behavior toward women.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, MARY CLARE JALONICK and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Department freezes new funding for nearly all US aid programs worldwide
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department ordered a sweeping freeze Friday on new funding for almost all U.S. foreign assistance, making exceptions for emergency food programs and military aid to Israel and Egypt. The order threatened a quick halt to many of the billions of dollars in U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NY Congresswoman Laura Gillen explains Laken Riley Act
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The new U.S. House of Representatives passed its first major, bipartisan legislation this week. The Laken Riley Act requires federal officials to detain undocumented immigrants if they are accused of theft.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says he wants to overhaul FEMA as he travels to North Carolina and California
item.description: President Trump is taking the first trip of his term on Friday to North Carolina and California, visiting communities grappling with recovery from natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: Asma Khalid and Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Experts warn about the ’crumbling infrastructure’ of federal government data
item.description: Unstable federal funding puts at risk the government statistics used to track the U.S. economy and population, officials and data users warn. That’s before any cuts by President Trump and Congress.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: Trump has tossed a lot of Biden policies. But there’s one AI measure he wants to keep
item.description: President Trump says he likes Biden’s idea to open up federal lands for AI data centers. His White House is looking for ways to ensure U.S. dominance in the sector.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Trump orders release of JFK, RFK and MLK assassination records
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered the release of thousands of classified governmental documents about the 1963 assassination of President John F. Kennedy, which has fueled conspiracy theories for decades.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump pardons anti-abortion activists who blockaded clinic entrances
item.description: President Donald Trump announced Thursday he would pardon anti-abortion activists convicted of blockading abortion clinic entrances.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: The new Justice Department leadership has put a freeze on civil rights litigation, and suggested it may reconsider police reform agreements negotiated by the Biden administration. That's according to two memos obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump calls DEI programs ‘illegal’ and ‘immoral.’ Here’s how he’s ending them
item.description: President Donald Trump has issued sweeping executive actions swiftly ending diversity, equity, inclusion and accessibility programs within the federal government. Already, the work is underway.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Everything Trump did in the first executive orders and actions of his presidency
item.description: President Donald Trump began his second administration with a blitz of policy actions to reorient U.S. government priorities. Some have an immediate policy impact. Others are more symbolic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: NJ bill looks to eliminate hurdle for student service members
item.description: FLORHAM PARK, N.J. (PIX11) - From studying to serving: that was National Guard veteran Frank "Doc" Schupp's life in the late 2000s. He was called to deploy to Iraq at the end of his last semester of his junior year at Kean University, in the midst of prepping for finals.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: House passes Laken Riley Act, sending it to Trump for first legislative win
item.description: The House on Wednesday passed the Laken Riley Act, sending the immigration-related bill to President Trump’s desk in what is poised to be his first legislative victory since returning to the White House this week. The chamber cleared the bill in a 263-156 vote.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump pumped the brakes on his plan to deliver wide-ranging tariffs as soon as he took office, slow-tracking and toning down changes to the U.S. trade system that figured as a centerpiece of his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Inauguration prayer service's pastor asks Trump to 'have mercy' on transgender kids, immigrants
item.description: “I ask you to have mercy upon the people in our country who are scared. There are gay, lesbian, transgender children, Democratic, Republican, independent families — some who fear for their lives,” she said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams says he won't publicly criticize President Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams said Tuesday he would not criticize President Donald Trump publicly. Adams also doggedly defended his decision to attend Trump’s inauguration Monday and skip local Martin Luther King Jr.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Examining Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump attends prayer service at National Cathedral
item.description: After being sworn in on Monday, President Donald Trump marked his first full day in office with a prayer service at Washington National Cathedral on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, Fox 5

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Michelle Obama not attending Donald Trump's inauguration: officials
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The former first lady of the U.S., Michelle Obama, isn't attending President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration on Monday, according to her office. It's not clear why she's not at the swearing-in ceremony. Her husband, former President Barack Obama, is taking part in the event.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11
